title: Odontostemma bomiense
taxonomy: Odontostemma bomiense is a species of plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a flowering plant native to China. The classification of Odontostemma bomiense includes its genus, Odontostemma, and its order, Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Odontostemma bomiense is also classified as a monocot, meaning it has one cotyledon or seed leaf in its embryo. Further classification of Odontostemma bomiense includes its family, order, class, and kingdom, which are all part of the plant's taxonomic hierarchy.

intro: Odontostemma bomiense, also known as Bomi Odontostemma, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the primrose family (Primulaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Bomi Odontostemma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Bomi Odontostemma is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, as well as being incorporated into patent medicines like capsules and tablets.
